audit_items.proof_trace: All dynamic allocations in nfp_cpplib.c are properly freed on every path. nfp_cpp_explicit_read and nfp_cpp_explicit_write acquire a kzalloc'd handle via nfp_cpp_explicit_acquire; every code path (success and all error branches) reaches the exit_release label which calls nfp_cpp_explicit_release -> kfree. nfp_cpp_map_area frees the area on all internal error paths via nfp_cpp_area_release_free, and on success the caller is documented to call nfp_cpp_area_release_free(*area) — this is an intentional API contract, not a leak; all other functions use only stack-allocated buffers.
